prose: The positive call rests on curated mechanistic convergence: rotenone appears as a CTD Parkinson's DirectEvidence entry and a registered neuro-AOP stressor [E1], which anchors this evidence-ranked mechanistic hypothesis. That chain is biologically grounded, with the molecular initiating event mapped to MitoCarta Complex-I Q-site subunits and the adverse outcome localized to the Kamath SOX6/AGTR1 vulnerable dopaminergic-neuron population [E2], offering a plausible cell-type-specific route from mitochondrial perturbation to selective neuronal loss. Brain-penetrance predictions suggest CNS exposure is structurally plausible [E5], and human cohort data report an elevated PD association (OR ~2.5) [E4], though such epidemiology is correlational and cannot establish causation. The 56 ToxCast assay hits [E3] illustrate but do not decide the mechanism; being anti-diagnostic, they serve only as corroboration, not as a discriminator. Together these strands support the assay_mechanism_recovered tier without implying that rotenone causes disease.

citations.detail: Curated convergence: CTD Parkinson's DirectEvidence AND registered neuro-AOP stressor.
MIE grounded in MitoCarta Complex-I Q-site subunits; adverse outcome in the Kamath SOX6/AGTR1 vulnerable dopaminergic-neuron population.
56 mechanistic assay hits illustrate the chain (corroboration only — anti-diagnostic, not a discriminator).
